papers_mined.title: Tuvan throat singing Wikipedia
papers_mined.note: Khoomei generic + 3 main styles khoomei/kargyraa/sygyt; sub-styles borbangnadyr/chylandyk/dumchuktaar/ezengileer/kanzyp; fundamental + simultaneously focused overtone.

papers_mined.title: Overtone focusing biphonic Tuvan throat singing eLife
papers_mined.note: Vocal tract manipulation merges 2 formants at 1-2 kHz; biphonic = fundamental + focused filter at higher pitch in narrow frequency window.

papers_mined.title: Khoomei Types Tips
papers_mined.note: Sygyt: tongue rises and seals around gums; small hole left behind molars on either side; sound directed between teeth, lips form bell with 'ee' vowel.

papers_mined.title: Alash Tuvan Throat Singing
papers_mined.note: Kargyraa uses vocal + vestibular folds simultaneously (2 connected sources); fundamental E or G below middle C; higher partials easier on lower fundamentals.
